unverified_claims.note: No primary keyword research tool (Keywords Everywhere, Ahrefs, Semrush) was directly queried. The 80K/mo estimate aggregates 'generative engine optimization' (lower volume, emerging term), 'AI SEO' (high volume), and 'AI search optimization' (moderate volume). Actual combined category search volume may be significantly higher if related queries (GEO marketing, AI search strategy, ChatGPT SEO) are included.

The 50.5% CAGR is a forward-looking forecast (2026–2034) from Intel Market Research, not a backward-looking 5-year historical figure. The GEO category as a distinct market segment did not meaningfully exist before 2023. Using forward CAGR as the best available proxy for market growth velocity, consistent with how the metric is used in other hemle.blog reports (see ai-chip-wars-trend-data.json). The actual observed 2024→2026 growth rate of ~94% is higher but covers only 2 years.

The 357% figure measures AI platform referral visits year-over-year as of June 2025 (Similarweb via BrightEdge), not the GEO services market's sequential quarterly growth. It is used as the most recent, authoritative short-term momentum indicator for the AI search category driving GEO demand. No sequential Q-o-Q GEO market revenue figure was available from a published source.

unverified_claims.claim: Perplexity market share described as 'Fast-growing' without a precise %
unverified_claims.note: Perplexity's exact AI chatbot/search market share percentage was not found in a primary source during this research session. The available data (ChatGPT 68%, Gemini 18.2%, Copilot 13.2%) accounts for ~99.4% of the AI chatbot market measured by Similarweb, leaving limited precise room for Perplexity in that specific dataset. Perplexity's share is real but measured differently (pure AI search vs. chatbot category). 'Fast-growing' is an accurate descriptor based on valuation and ARR trajectory.
